objective: Close the loop on the control-plane architecture so the system is not just a diagram or mapping, but a complete governed runtime: proposal -> packaging -> verification -> governance -> human authorization when required -> execution gate -> external action -> receipt/witness -> immutable ledger -> learning loop feedback into future policy and intent handling.
core_instruction: Do not explain the architecture back. Build the missing closure points so the loop is operational end to end.
what_close_the_loop_means: Every intent must enter as a structured envelope.
Every envelope must be verified before policy evaluation.
Every verified intent must receive a governance decision.
If risk/policy requires human approval, execution must halt until explicit approval is recorded.
No external action can occur without passing the execution gate.
Every executed action must generate a receipt/witness artifact.
Every receipt must be written to the immutable ledger.
Ledger records must feed a learning loop that updates policy suggestions, thresholds, or replay analytics without silently mutating production policy.
The system must prove chain-of-custody from intent through outcome.

formal_to_rio.required_behavior: Generate proposal only. No approval authority. No execution authority.
Wrap proposal with request_id, source, actor, timestamp, nonce, target, action_type, parameters, context, and integrity metadata.
Validate schema, signature if present, nonce freshness, timestamp TTL, source authenticity, required fields, and replay protection before governance.
Apply policy rules, risk scoring, compliance checks, and decision tiering: approve, deny, or require human authorization.
Record approver identity, decision, timestamp, and approval artifact. No implied approval. Silence equals refusal.
Re-check that approvals, policy state, token validity, TTL, and preconditions still hold immediately before execution.
Perform the real-world action only after successful gate clearance.
Capture proof of actual execution: action result, external response, status code, timestamps, hashes, verification status.
Append tamper-evident record linking intent, governance decision, approval record, execution record, and receipt.

Analyze outcomes from ledger data and produce policy recommendations, drift detection, threshold suggestions, and replay simulation inputs. No automatic policy mutation in production unless explicitly approved.

required_build_actions.title: Make intent envelope first-class
required_build_actions.why: Without a canonical intent envelope, there is no stable chain-of-custody.
implementation: Define a canonical intent envelope schema.
Include: intent_id, request_id, source_type, source_id, actor_id, timestamp, nonce, action_type, target, parameters, context, correlation_id, policy_version_target, signature_optional, metadata.
Canonicalize for hashing.

required_build_actions.title: Separate verification from governance
required_build_actions.why: Validity and policy are not the same thing. Broken or spoofed requests must fail before governance.
implementation: Create a verification step that runs before policy.
Reject malformed, expired, replayed, unsigned-when-required, or unauthorized requests.
Emit explicit verification result object.

required_build_actions.title: Formalize governance decision object
required_build_actions.why: The system needs a deterministic policy decision artifact before any action can continue.
implementation: Decision object must include: decision_id, intent_hash, policy_version, risk_score, risk_level, decision, reasons, required_approvals, blocking_conditions, timestamp.

required_build_actions.title: Enforce explicit human authorization boundary
required_build_actions.why: High-risk actions must not drift into execution through implied permission.
implementation: If governance says human approval required, halt execution.
Capture approver identity, approval payload, signed or authenticated approval artifact, timestamp, and decision rationale.
No approval artifact means no execution.

required_build_actions.title: Add execution token and final preflight gate
required_build_actions.why: Approval must be bound to the exact action being executed and must expire.
implementation: Issue single-use execution token after successful approval or auto-approval.
Bind token to intent hash, action hash, policy version, TTL, nonce, and target.
Execution gate must verify token before connector call.

required_build_actions.title: Produce receipt/witness artifact
required_build_actions.why: The loop is not closed until execution proof exists.
implementation: Create receipt with links to intent, governance, approval, execution, and verification artifacts.
Include timestamps, outcome, external response IDs, verification status, and hashes.
Prefer signed receipt if available.

required_build_actions.title: Append full chain to immutable ledger
required_build_actions.why: Proof must be tamper-evident and replayable.
implementation: Ledger entry should store receipt_hash, previous_ledger_hash, current_hash, block_index, timestamp.
Append only. No overwrite.
Recompute chain validation in tests.

required_build_actions.title: Close learning loop safely
required_build_actions.why: The system must improve from outcomes without silently changing policy.
implementation: Build analytics job over ledger and receipts.
Generate reports for false positives, false negatives, approval bottlenecks, execution failures, policy misses, and repeated override patterns.
Output policy recommendations or replay inputs.
Do not auto-change live policy unless explicit approved promotion path exists.

required_runtime_rules: High-risk or policy-flagged actions require explicit human approval.
Execution token must be single-use and time-bound.
Execution must be denied if token, policy version, or preconditions do not match.
Every external action must emit receipt data.
Every receipt must be appended to ledger.
Learning loop may recommend changes but cannot silently mutate production policy.
definition_of_done: A single intent can be traced end to end from proposal to receipt.
The system blocks execution when verification fails.
The system blocks execution when policy requires approval and no approval exists.
The system binds approval to the exact intended action.
The system emits a verifiable receipt after execution.
The system appends a tamper-evident ledger entry.
The system runs a learning analysis over completed records.
A demo or test proves the full closed loop in one flow.

design_principles: No component may both approve and execute the same action.
Learning is advisory until explicitly promoted.

deliverables: Updated JSON schemas for all core artifacts
Implementation of missing runtime boundary checks
End-to-end test covering full closed loop
One demo flow proving proposal -> approval -> execution -> receipt -> ledger -> learning output
Short build note stating exactly what was added to close the loop
do_not_do: Do not collapse verification and governance into one step.
Do not let execution happen before explicit approval when approval is required.
Do not let learning loop auto-edit production policy without a separate approval path.
Do not describe this abstractly without implementing the missing closure points.
Do not rely on UI-only approval; bind approval cryptographically or with authenticated backend record.
single_sentence_summary: Close the loop by making every action provably traverse structured intent, verification, governance, authorization, gated execution, receipt generation, immutable ledger storage, and safe learning feedback.
